title: The Best Defensive Strategies: Two Centuries of Evidence
abstract: This paper tests defensive overlays on a 60/40 portfolio across 220+ years (1800-2021) of global financial history covering 8 severe drawdown episodes. The clearest winners are: (1) Defensive Absolute Return (DAR4020) — long factors with negative 60/40 correlation, short positive-correlation factors — returning +1.0%/month during the worst 10% of market months with 68% hit rate; and (2) 12-month trend-following across stocks, bonds, currencies, and commodities — returning +0.5%/month with 58% hit rate. A 50/50 DAR+trend combination reduced the eight largest 60/40 drawdowns from an average of -37.6% to -15.1%. Traditional equity factors (low-risk, quality, value) provided consistent downside protection. Gold returned -0.4%/month during crises; put options cost -2.5%/year; Treasuries unreliable.
authors: Elisabetta Basilico, PhD, CFA (summarising Guido Baltussen, Martin Martens, and Lodewijk van der Linden, FAJ 2025)
body:
Original paper: 'The Best Defensive Strategies: Two Centuries of Evidence' by Guido Baltussen, Martin Martens, and Lodewijk van der Linden, Financial Analysts Journal Vol 82, No 1 (2025). SSRN: https://papers.ssrn.com/sol3/papers.cfm?abstract_id=5815464

Alpha Architect blog post: March 9, 2026. Full summary also available at: https://larryswedroe.substack.com/p/the-best-defense-what-222-years-of

What the researchers examined:
The dataset spans 1800-2021, capturing 8 instances where the 60/40 portfolio lost more than 20%, plus hundreds of smaller downturns. Defensive strategies tested:
- Traditional defensive equity factors: low-risk stocks, quality companies, value stocks (dividend yield)
- Gold
- 12-month trend-following (stocks, bonds, currencies, commodities)
- Treasury bonds
- Equity put options
- Defensive Absolute Return (DAR): factors sorted by historical correlation with 60/40 portfolio

Key Quantitative Results:

1. DAR4020 (Defensive Absolute Return, 40% long / 20% short):  
   - Returns +1.0%/month during worst 10% of market months
   - Effective 68% of the time during drawdowns
   - Provides immediate protection at drawdown onset (pre-positioned negative beta)
   - Example: COVID-19 crash (Feb-Mar 2020) — DAR4020 returned +5.0% while 60/40 lost -12.4%

2. 12-Month Trend-Following (multi-asset):  
   - Returns +0.5%/month during worst market periods
   - Effective 58% of the time
   - Needs time to reposition; performs better in extended downturns
   - Example: COVID-19 crash — trend-following returned -1.1% (too fast for repositioning)

3. Combined DAR4020 + Trend-Following (50/50):  
   - Reduced 8 largest 60/40 drawdowns from average -37.6% to -15.1%
   - Strategies complement each other across drawdown stages

4. Traditional Equity Factors:
   - Low-risk stocks: consistently positive during market stress
   - Quality companies: consistently positive during market stress  
   - Value (dividend yield): consistently positive during market stress
   - All outperformed gold and put options, though underperformed DAR4020 and trend-following

5. Gold: Returns -0.4%/month during worst 60/40 periods over full 220-year sample (despite positive performance in 2008 crisis specifically)

6. Put options: -2.5%/year all-in cost; 'prohibitively costly' for ongoing protection

7. Treasury bonds: -3.5% cumulative return over cash during 12 largest equity drawdowns since 1800; too dependent on stock-bond correlation regime

Key Conclusion: The most reliable defensive overlays are DAR4020 and trend-following, which are complementary. Traditional equity factors (low-risk, quality, value) provide meaningful but less powerful protection. Gold and put options do NOT reliably protect over long horizons.

Note: Authors did not model transaction costs, which matter especially for high-turnover strategies like trend-following.
